title: verbascum lobatum
intro: Verbascum lobatum, also known as Mullein, is a biennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its large, velvety leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The leaves and flowers of the Mullein plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. The medicinal properties of Mullein have been shown to provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and allergies, as well as skin irritations and wounds. Mullein can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile remedy for various health benefits and conditions.
